title: boraginella incana
intro: Boraginella incana is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae, commonly known as the hairy borage or hairy blue thistle. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Boraginella incana include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Boraginella incana is typically prepared as a tea or infusion, and it can also be made into a tincture or used as a topical cream for its medicinal benefits.
